---
title: "Analyse-Notebook"
author: "Jinwook Bae, Miriam Haf, Xuchen Shen, Andreas Sünder"
lang: de
format: html
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
source("source_all.R")
download_data()

COUNTRIES <- c("Germany", "Italy", "Sweden")
```

# Analysis of education data

## School to work transition

```{r}
# stwt <- load_ilo_stwt()
# # stwt[, `:=`(indicator.label = NULL, note_source.label = NULL)]
# setnames(stwt, c("ref_area.label", "sex.label", "classif1.label", "time", "obs_value"), c("country", "sex", "classifier", "year", "value"))
# 
# data <- stwt[year == 2023 & sex != "Total" & classifier == "Status in employment (ICSE-93): 1. Employees", .(year, country, classifier, sex, value)]
# 
# View(data[, , by = country])
# 
# ggplot(data[country %in% c("Germany", "Italy", "Sweden", "Mexico", "Jordan")], aes(x = country, y = value, fill = sex)) +
#   geom_bar(position = "fill", stat = "identity")
```

## Completion rates

```{r}
completion <- load_uis_completion_rates()

completion[order(-year), .N, by = year]

# Global completion
completion.global <- completion[year == 2022, .(value = sum(value)), by = .(indicator, sex)]
ggplot(completion.global, aes(x = indicator, y = value, fill = sex)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge", stat = "identity")

# by country
completion.countries <- completion[country %in% COUNTRIES]
ggplot(completion.countries[year == 2020], aes(x = country, y = value, fill = sex)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~indicator, ncol = 1)

# over time (averaged over all countries)
completion.global_time <- completion[, .(value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = .(year, indicator, sex)]
ggplot(completion.global_time, aes(x = year, y = value, color = sex, linetype = indicator)) +
  geom_line()
```

## Youth literacy

```{r}
literacy <- load_uis_literacy_rates()

literacy[order(-year), .N, by = year]

literacy.uis <- literacy[age_group == "15-24" & grepl("^UIS:", geoUnit)]
literacy.uis[, region := sub("^UIS: ", "", geoUnit)]

literacy.gap <- dcast(literacy.uis,
  region + year ~ sex, value.var = "value"
)[, gap := Male - Female]

# Use most recent year available per region
literacy.gap.latest <- literacy.gap[!is.na(gap), .SD[which.max(year)], by = region]

# Globally (most recent year available)
ggplot(literacy.gap.latest, aes(x = reorder(region, gap), y = gap)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Gender gap (male - female, pp)")

# over time
ggplot(literacy.gap, aes(x = year, y = gap, color = region)) +
     geom_line()
```
